reasoning: The FRED statistical model predicts a 0.66 probability of the effective yield being higher on Nov 30 based on recent upward linear trend since mid-October (from 6.56 to 6.81). However, very short-term momentum is slightly negative, dropping from 6.89 on Nov 18 to 6.81 on Nov 21. Macro commentary indicates elevated Treasury yields due to sticky inflation and a strong economy, potentially keeping high-yield credit yields anchored or gently rising in the short term. Over the longer term, expected Federal Reserve rate cuts in late 2025 and early 2026 will put downward pressure on underlying Treasury yields, although stable credit spreads could dampen the overall decline. To account for increasing uncertainty over time, probabilities are drifted toward 0.5 for the later resolution dates.
